executive_summary: The Iran-US conflict has escalated dramatically with Iran's Supreme Leader killed in an Israeli strike on March 2, triggering expanded Iranian retaliatory operations across the Gulf region. Six US soldiers have been confirmed killed in Kuwait, with IRGC launching attacks on US bases in Jordan, UAE, and Kuwait using drones and ballistic missiles. The war is projected by Trump to last 4 weeks, while Iran has closed the Strait of Hormuz and launched drone attacks on the US embassy in Riyadh.
whats_new: IRGC closed the Strait of Hormuz, threatening global oil supplies (confirmed by multiple sources)
US Army confirms 6 soldiers killed in Kuwait missile strike - no alarm was activated before impact
UAE Defense Ministry: air defenses intercepting ballistic missile volley launched directly from Iran
US embassy in Riyadh attacked by two drones - escalation against US diplomatic facility in Gulf
Israeli-US 'double-tap' strike on downtown Tehran targeted rescuers - potential war crime
IRGC announced striking 50 strategic targets and 500 military points using 700 drones and hundreds of missiles
Qatar filed formal UN complaint over Iranian attack on its territory
US State Department urged all Americans to immediately leave 15 Middle Eastern countries
confirmed_events: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei killed in Israeli strike on Tehran - confirmed by Trump statement and multiple sources
Six US service members killed in first three days of Iran-US war - confirmed by US military announcement
Iranian ballistic missile directly struck Muwaffaq Salti Air Base in Jordan - visual confirmation of impact
Israeli airstrikes on Tehran and Beirut (Hezbollah command) - ongoing operations
Iranian missiles successfully evaded US air defense systems in Kuwait's airspace
unverified_emerging: IRGC claims killing dozens of US officers and commanders in Dubai (IRGC source only - unverified)
IRGC claims attack on gathering of 160+ US Marine infantry personnel in UAE (IRGC source only)
Norwegian rights group Hengaw reports day-three death toll in western Iran reaching at least 1,500 (single source, cannot independently verify)

people_travel: US State Department issued 'immediate departure' advisory for 15 Middle Eastern countries including Saudi Arabia, UAE, Bahrain, Iran, Iraq, Jordan, Kuwait, Lebanon, Qatar
Airspace restrictions and flight cancellations strand travelers across region - Trump advisor reported being 'stuck in the Middle East'
supply_chain: Strait of Hormuz closed by IRGC - critical global oil chokepoint disrupted
Asia-Pacific markets extend losses as Iran conflict rages; oil prices rising
US-allied fuel tanker attacked and still burning in Strait of Hormuz
market_macro: South Korean defense stocks surge: Hanwha Aerospace jumps 22%
Broader Kospi index falls 2%, reflecting divergent market sentiment
Global oil supply threat from Hormuz closure driving price increases

outlook_24_72h.base_case: Conflict will intensify over the next 24-72 hours with Iran continuing retaliatory strikes across Gulf states (UAE, Kuwait, Jordan, Qatar) and against Israeli territory. IRGC has signaled sustained missile and drone campaigns targeting US bases and personnel. Trump projects 4-week timeline, suggesting sustained operations rather than rapid resolution. Civilian casualties (200+ confirmed in Iran, 6 US soldiers dead) will drive continued escalation cycle.
escalation_risks: IRGC threatens strikes on 50 strategic targets using 700 drones - massed attack phase likely imminent
Iran targeting nuclear facilities (Natanz) - escalates to nuclear infrastructure dimension
Gulf states (UAE, Kuwait, Jordan, Qatar) now directly in Iranian strike crossfire - risks drawing regional powers into conflict
US Congress Democrats pushing war powers vote - potential domestic political constraint on military operations
Hezbollah deepening involvement with rockets from Tulkarm toward Israel
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: No visible de-escalation pathways. Iran's Supreme Leader killing removes moderate leadership constraint. IRGC operations expanding geographically. Trump administration shows no indication of halting operations. Regional powers (Qatar, UAE, Jordan) filing UN complaints but not militarily intervening. Indian PM Modi diplomatic outreach indicates international pressure building but insufficient to halt hostilities.
